what_it_does: This file performs an adversarial source-check verification pass over palette-related design-token claims, flipping a default verdict of REFUTED to STANDS only when an opened source slide visibly shows the rule, or to CORRECTED when the rule is real but the recipe mismatches the pixels. It records opened slides, PIL median color samples, per-claim verdicts, bias flags, evidence, corrected recipes, and a headline summary.
observations: Default verdict is REFUTED; evidence must come from opened source slides, not the proposal's claims about live files
Confirming-bias claims such as extends-existing / already correct are flagged and graded strictly on source slides
PIL median samples are cited for specific slides and elements, e.g., #B5C145 olive-gold $ badge ring, #F89D12 outcome bars, #AD8E69/#B29135/#B18D36 muted golds
The semantic_rare.transaction green-teal $ badge claim is refuted; the badge is olive-gold (#B5C145) with no green present
boundary-maroon is corrected to apply only to the p-09 zone border, not the dark hub node, which belongs to the axis_ramp terminus
The <=3-5 gold-instance budget is corrected to an austere-pole quantification only, not a universal slide-measured limit
The ordinal warm-metal ramp pattern stands, but the claim that the live face already renders it correctly is flagged unproven against a flat measurement
directional green/red (p-26) and financial-outcome orange (p-15) are confirmed
white_depth blank-white and the 3-depth ladder are confirmed from overlay chrome and cream-ground cards
watermark is resolved to a texture token rather than a new color
chart_ramp is confirmed as a warm-analogous data-viz ramp with grey as constraint
standouts: semantic_rare.transaction is refuted: the cited green-teal $ badge is actually olive-gold (#B5C145)
boundary-maroon is corrected: real as a zone border, but wrongly folded into the dark bronze hub node
figure-gold value conflict stands: source-true muted golds (~#AD8E69-#B29135) versus live bright #E3C421
directional green/red and financial-outcome orange are strongly confirmed
white_depth and chart_ramp are confirmed as new structural patterns
conventions: Adversarial default-refuted posture with explicit source-evidence requirement
Each verdict names the claim, status_claimed, verdict, confirming_bias flag, source_evidence, and corrected_recipe
Evidence is anchored to specific slide/page references and, where relevant, PIL hex samples
Counts and a narrative headline are provided at the end
Status labels use STANDS / CORRECTED / REFUTED
concerns: Some verdicts mix measured pixel values with inferred semantic roles and deployment rules
The confirming_bias flag is asserted without showing how it was detected or scored
The n_stands/n_refuted/n_corrected counts appear manually tallied and could drift if verdicts change
No timestamp, author, or version of the source slide set is recorded
Several verdicts rely on synthesis of predicate strings whose exact grammar is not defined in this file
notes: A pixel-sampled, slide-backed audit of palette-token claims that defaults to refutation and explicitly corrects overreach rather than auto-approving existing or extending claims.
questions: Which live file or token registry is being compared against the source slides?
Who is Tim and what decision was recorded for the figure-gold value conflict?
What is product-vi-landing and how does its guidance relate to the slide measurements?
How were the specific slides and page numbers selected for opening?
What schema governs the underlying ledger entries and token names?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is an adversarial source-check of palette claims backed by opened slides and pixel samples; the file does exactly that, producing verdicts, evidence, corrections, and a summary.
apparent_themes: Semantic color economy and rare-hue quarantine
Warm-metal ordinal ramp as attention/proximity/time encoding
Source-grounded design tokens with pixel verification
intention_signals: It appears designed to stop design-token drift by forcing every rule to prove itself on source slides, surfacing conflicts openly and correcting overreach instead of rubber-stamping extensions.
novelty: The default-refuted stance combined with confirming-bias tagging and pixel-sampled hex corrections is unusual; it treats extends-existing / already-correct claims as the prime suspect rather than safe.
contribution: A structured, source-evidenced audit trail for palette decisions, including explicit refutations, corrected recipes, slide-cited evidence, and pixel measurements that a fused system can use to reconcile token claims without treating any version as canonical.
summary_for_embedding: A verified ledger entry for the palette face that adversarially evaluates color and semantic-token claims against opened source slides and PIL pixel samples, returning STANDS, CORRECTED, or REFUTED verdicts with slide-cited evidence.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a bias-resistant, source-grounded verification pass for palette design-substrate claims before they are accepted into the live token system.
